about_text: Since 2008, developing software solutions focused on <mark class="inline-block py-0 px-3 rounded-full font-semibold text-gray-600 bg-gray-200">quality</mark>, <mark class="inline-block py-0 px-3 rounded-full font-semibold text-gray-600 bg-gray-200">reliability</mark> and constant <mark class="inline-block py-0 px-3 rounded-full font-semibold text-gray-600 bg-gray-200">improvement</mark>. I started my career with websites and, over the years, expanded my work into different areas of technology.
goals_text: To contribute to innovative, continuously improving software development projects, always aiming to deliver stable, efficient, and high-quality solutions.
agrotools_description: Working on the development of agribusiness applications, focusing on robust, scalable solutions built on modern architecture.
agrotools_bullet_1: Development of systems using .NET and .NET Framework, creating integrated, high-performance solutions;
agrotools_bullet_2: Creation and maintenance of RESTful APIs and modern interfaces using JavaScript and Vue 3;
agrotools_bullet_3: Definition and adjustment of CI/CD pipelines with Azure DevOps, automating the delivery cycle;
agrotools_bullet_4: Building and maintaining routines with Azure LogicApps, integrating processes and services;
agrotools_bullet_5: Extensive use of PostgreSQL with the PostGIS extension for geospatial data manipulation applied to territorial analysis;
agrotools_bullet_6: Working with Docker and Kubernetes for container creation, packaging, and orchestration in distributed environments, ensuring scalability and consistency in the runtime environment.
agrotools_summary: This experience has reinforced my role as a full-stack developer in projects with a strong presence of geospatial data, automation, cloud computing, and microservices.
sardo_description: Working on the development of agribusiness applications, focusing on robust, scalable solutions built on modern architecture.
sardo_bullet_1: Responsible for parameterization, configuration, and user support across various modules of the ERP Senior, including billing, manufacturing, purchasing, HR and accounting;
sardo_bullet_2: Development of a custom module within the Senior ERP for export process management, controlling all stages of the export workflow;
sardo_bullet_3: Direct client support in fiscal, operational, and administrative routines, ensuring adherence to business rules through system adjustments and improvements;
sardo_bullet_4: Creation of a dashboard in PHP 7.4, connected to the ERP's SQL Server, enabling real-time production monitoring and goal tracking;
sardo_bullet_5: Development of a quality inspection system for the timber sector, using PHP 7.4 and MySQL, enabling the logging of field sample photos and measurements.
sardo_summary: This experience has reinforced my role as a full-stack developer in projects with a strong presence of geospatial data, automation, cloud computing, and microservices.
isopack_description: I joined the company as IT support during the rollout of a new ERP, and over time took on broader responsibilities involving infrastructure, system development, and solution integration.
isopack_bullet_1: Direct support to the ERP implementation team, receiving training, performing initial configurations, and guiding the company's adaptation to the new system;
isopack_bullet_2: Providing technical user support, computer maintenance, local network, backups and IT infrastructure management;
isopack_bullet_3: Working with Linux servers for ERP hosting, with IBM DB2 database administration;
isopack_bullet_4: Development of ERP extensions using PHP (5.6 and 7.4) and MySQL, covering functionality not native to the system;
isopack_bullet_5: Creation of a CRM-style application for customer service, developed in PHP 5.6 with MySQL;
isopack_bullet_6: Development of an EPS production management system, including inventory control, machine downtime, raw material consumption, and product dispatch, using PHP 7.4 and MySQL.
isopack_summary: This experience consolidated my transition from technical support to systems development, focusing on solutions aligned with the company's operational needs.
luani_description: Multifunctional role at a stationery and party supply store, with direct involvement in customer service, inventory, sales, and — most importantly — first interactions with technology and e-commerce, which sparked my interest in web development.
luani_bullet_1: Responsible for updating banners and products on the company website via a control panel, back when the site was in its institutional format;
luani_bullet_2: Support in the launch of the online store: product registration and updates, inventory management, order processing, shipping logistics, and customer service;
luani_bullet_3: Creation of a WordPress blog for the e-commerce site, producing content and integrating with social media (Facebook and Twitter);
luani_bullet_4: Customer-facing service, direct sales, product organization, and shelf restocking;
luani_bullet_5: Product registration in the point-of-sale system, pricing based on invoices, labeling, and physical inventory control;
luani_bullet_6: Logistics support for customer deliveries, contributing to the full operation of the business.
luani_summary: This experience was fundamental in sparking my interest in technology and understanding all the operational stages of a business.
freelance_intro: I worked on various freelance projects, developing customized solutions for partners and small businesses. The work ranged from institutional websites to on-demand web systems, with a focus on quality, usability, and agile delivery.
bc_description: System for managing the production of EPS (expanded polystyrene) blocks and molded pieces, with machine downtime tracking and inventory movement control.
fd_description: Order and production management system for a healthy meals company. Includes features for kitchen management, deliveries, customers, products, and delivery drivers.
bf_description: Platform for managing benefactors and their contributions to a religious formation house. Complete control of registrations, donations, and interactions with donors.
unisul_degree: Technologist in Web Design and Programming
final_item_2: Quick learner of new technologies and processes
final_item_4: Always willing to take on new challenges

project_bc_description: System for managing the production of EPS (expanded polystyrene) blocks and molded pieces, with machine downtime tracking and inventory movement control.
project_bc_g1_subtitle: Login screen of the Business Control system.
project_bc_g2_subtitle: Home dashboard with various summarized information.
project_bc_why_1: Business Control was born out of a real operational need. The company used a generic ERP that did not meet the specifics of the EPS (expanded polystyrene) block and molded piece production process. The most critical controls — such as production records, machine downtime, inventory entries, and internal movement — were handled manually in Excel spreadsheets.
project_bc_why_2: This model presented several problems: lack of traceability of what was being produced, difficulty seeing up-to-date inventory status, and little visibility into ongoing production. In other words, daily operations relied on parallel, decentralized controls prone to human error.
project_bc_why_3: Business Control was developed to solve exactly that: replace manual control with a dedicated system aligned with the factory's actual workflow. The application centralized tracking of EPS block and molded piece production, structured inventory movement records, and provided a clear view of what had been produced, what was in production, and what was available. This brought greater control, more reliable data, and a solid foundation for decision-making.
project_fd_description: Order and production management system for a healthy meals company. Includes features for kitchen management, deliveries, customers, products, and delivery drivers.
project_fd_g1_subtitle: Login screen of the Fast Delivery system.
project_fd_g2_subtitle: Home dashboard with various summarized information.
project_fd_g3_subtitle: Production dashboard showing the quantities to be produced.
project_fd_g4_subtitle: Dashboard showing meals ready to be packaged, sorted by delivery driver.
project_fd_g5_subtitle: Dashboard showing deliveries to be made by each driver, organized by delivery sequence.
project_fd_why_1: Fast Delivery was created to meet the specific needs of a healthy meals company looking to optimize its order and production process. Before the system was developed, the company faced significant challenges in order management, production control, and delivery logistics, which negatively impacted operational efficiency and customer satisfaction.
project_fd_why_2: With Fast Delivery, the company was able to centralize all operations on a single platform. The system enables efficient order management from receipt to delivery, while also providing tools to control kitchen production, manage ingredient inventory, and coordinate delivery drivers. This led to a significant improvement in internal organization, reduced errors and delays, and a more satisfying experience for customers.
project_bf_description: Platform for managing benefactors and their contributions to a religious formation house. Complete control of registrations, donations, and interactions with donors.
project_bf_g1_subtitle: Login screen of the Benefactors system.
project_bf_g2_subtitle: Home dashboard with various summarized information.
project_bf_why_1: The Benefactors platform was developed to meet the specific needs of a religious formation house seeking to improve the management of its benefactors and their contributions. Before the system was implemented, the institution faced challenges in organizing benefactor records, tracking donations, and maintaining effective relationships with donors.
project_bf_why_2: With Benefactors, the religious formation house was able to centralize all benefactor-related information on a single platform. The system enables efficient record management, detailed donation tracking, and easier interactions with donors. This resulted in a significant improvement in internal organization, greater transparency in received contributions, and a strengthened bond between the institution and its benefactors.
